action.reasoning: Observation: Persistent 'Image features and image tokens do not match (tokens: 240, features: 240)' on first forward despite matching counts in prior debugs; train succeeds up to model forward but fails due to VL input prep (likely template/image_processor mismatch on placeholder count vs grid_thw features from high-res screenshots). No successful eval after 19 iters. test_prep confirms good data with abs-pixel bbox/point. predict.py looks complete with parsers/rescale/clip/defaults/JSON. Analysis: Current messages include PIL image obj (may interfere with template), fixed max_pixels=256*28*28 still produces variable patches causing internal get_placeholder_mask failure in Qwen2VL; truncation in reports but code runs to forward. Root fix: messages without embedded PIL (use type:image only), add targeted debug prints for step 0 only (shapes, grid_thw, num_pad_tokens vs features), force consistent small res via min/max_pixels=128*28*28 + optional resize for stability (coords use original w/h so OK for relative learning), compact code to avoid any truncation risk, manual bs=1 loop with full tensor prep. This follows standard Qwen2VL SFT for grounding and will yield first successful train + checkpoint. Decision: Overwrite train.py (compact+debug+fixes, 32 samples/16 steps ~20s), keep predict+test_prep, run test_prep+train then submit_eval for first grounding_score + time before scaling data/epochs/LR.
